name: sf-note
description: Binary-assertion test suite for /sf:note. Validates pin-append, session-id resolution, fallback, refusal on empty text.

tests.prompt: /sf:note Stripe webhook signing is HMAC-SHA256 not raw SHA256
tests.expected_output_summary: Bullet appended to current session's notes file; user sees confirmation with path.
binary_assertions: The user-provided text appears verbatim in the file
The bullet starts with '- [' and includes an ISO-8601 UTC timestamp
User-facing output contains the substring 'Pinned to' followed by the file path

tests.prompt: /sf:note remember to check the rate limit on the auth endpoint
tests.expected_output_summary: Skill detects no resolvable session id, falls back to unsessioned-notes.md.
binary_assertions: The text appears verbatim in unsessioned-notes.md
User-facing output mentions 'unsessioned' or names the unsessioned file

tests.expected_output_summary: Skill refuses with a usage prompt; no file written.
binary_assertions: No new file is created under wiki/.session-notes/

tests.prompt: /sf:note second pin in the same session
tests.expected_output_summary: Appends to the existing file rather than overwriting or duplicating the header.
binary_assertions: The file contains exactly one '# Session notes' header line
Both the first and second pin texts appear in the file
The second pin's bullet appears AFTER the first (chronological order)

non_triggers.prompt: /sf:recall what did we decide about auth
